reason: The text contains a clear prophecy by Ahijah the prophet (1 Kings 14:15) stating that God will smite Israel, uproot them from their good land, and scatter them beyond the river as punishment for their idolatry. The prophecy uses the phrase 'And the Lord shall smite Israel, as a reed is shaken in the water, and he shall root up Israel out of this good land...and shall scatter them beyond the river.' This is explicitly a supernatural destruction by Yahweh (not individual but national in scope) that will affect the entire people of Israel. The destruction is presented as a future consequence of the nation's sins in building idolatrous groves and provoking God to anger.
